differential_diagnoses: Generalized herpes simplex virus infection (Kaposi’s varicelliform eruption) with superinfection
Drug reaction with eosinophilia and systemic symptoms (DRESS)
next_steps: Excisional biopsy of enlarged inguinal lymph node for histopathology and flow cytometry
T-cell receptor gene rearrangement studies on skin and lymph node specimens
Peripheral blood flow cytometry for Sézary cell identification
Complete body imaging (PET/CT) to better characterize the pulmonary mass and nodal disease
Serial monitoring of complete blood count and eosinophil count
Optimize wound care and continue targeted antibiotic therapy for P. aeruginosa

differential_diagnoses: Metastatic pulmonary malignancy with paraneoplastic erythroderma
Bullous pemphigoid with Kaposi's varicelliform eruption
Hyper-IgE syndrome (Job's syndrome) or idiopathic hyper-IgE state
Psoriatic erythroderma complicated by opportunistic infection
next_steps: Biopsy of pulmonary mass via CT-guided core needle aspiration or bronchoscopy
Excisional biopsy of enlarged inguinal lymph node for histopathology, flow cytometry, and T-cell receptor gene rearrangement
Peripheral blood flow cytometry for aberrant T-cell populations (Sézary cell assessment)
Skin biopsy of a fresh, non-ulcerated lesion for deeper immunohistochemistry and molecular studies
Complete body PET/CT imaging to evaluate hypermetabolic activity of the pulmonary mass and lymphadenopathy
Evaluation for bullous pemphigoid via direct and indirect immunofluorescence
